title: stevia veronicae var. tenuis
intro: Stevia veronicae var. tenuis, also known as Stevia tenuifolia, is a plant species of the genus Stevia, native to the subtropical regions of South America, and is a perennial herb that belongs to the sunflower family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to inhibit the growth of certain microorganisms and its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, fever, and skin problems, and its health benefits include lowering blood pressure, improving digestion, and reducing inflammation. Stevia tenuifolia can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and capsules, and it is also used as a natural sweetener, although its use as a food additive is still being researched and regulated.
